recommended_questions: How long have you had fever?
What is your current body temperature?
Do you have cough? If so, is it dry or with phlegm?
Any breathing difficulty or chest pain?
Have you been in contact with anyone who was sick?
Did you receive the flu vaccine this year?
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
Are you taking any medications?
When did your symptoms start?
Have you tried any medications? If so, which ones?
treatments: paracetamol or ibuprofen for fever and pain
antiviral medications like oseltamivir if within 48 hours of onset
monitor for warning signs of complications

recommended_questions: Where exactly is the pain located?
How severe is the pain on a scale of 1-10?
Does the pain throb or is it constant?
How long do your headaches typically last?
How often do you get these headaches?
Do you experience any warning signs before the headache starts?
Do you have sensitivity to light or sound during the headache?
Do you experience any visual changes?
Have you had these headaches before?
What triggers your headaches?
What medications have you tried for this?
Do you have any family history of migraines?
Are you under any unusual stress?
Has there been any recent change in your sleep pattern?
treatments: triptans such as sumatriptan or zolmitriptan
rest in a dark, quiet room
prophylactic medications like propranolol for frequent migraines

recommended_questions: How long have you had this cough?
Are you coughing up any sputum? What color is it?
Do you have any chest pain? Where exactly?
Does the pain change with breathing or coughing?
Are you experiencing shortness of breath?
What is your current temperature?
Have you had any recent respiratory infections?
Do you smoke?
Do you have any chronic lung conditions?
Have you been exposed to anyone with respiratory illness?
Do you have any medical conditions like diabetes or heart disease?
Are you on any medications?
Have you had any recent surgery or hospitalization?
Any night sweats or weight loss recently?
treatments: antibiotics appropriate to the causative organism
oxygen therapy if oxygen saturation is low
fever management with paracetamol or ibuprofen

recommended_questions: How long have you had this persistent cough?
Are you coughing up blood or blood-tinged sputum?
Do you have night sweats that drench your clothes?
Have you experienced unintended weight loss? How much?
When did you start feeling unwell?
Do you have any chest pain? When does it occur?
Have you had fever? What is your temperature?
Have you been exposed to anyone diagnosed with TB?
Do you live in crowded conditions?
Do you have HIV/AIDS or immunosuppression?
Do you have diabetes or other chronic diseases?
Are you on any immunosuppressive medications?
Have you traveled to high TB incidence areas?
What work do you do? Any occupational exposures?
treatments: regular monitoring for drug side effects
contact tracing and prophylaxis for exposed persons

recommended_questions: When did your symptoms start?
What did you eat in the last 24-48 hours?
Where did you eat the meal that you suspect caused this?
Have other people who ate the same food become sick?
What symptoms are you experiencing?
How many times have you vomited?
How many times have you had diarrhea?
Is the diarrhea watery or bloody?
Are you experiencing abdominal pain? Where?
Do you have fever? What is your temperature?
Are you able to keep fluids down?
Do you have any underlying medical conditions?
Are you on any medications?
Have you recently traveled outside your usual area?
Do you have any signs of severe dehydration?
treatments: oral rehydration therapy with water and electrolytes
gradual return to mild foods like crackers and broth
probiotics may help restore gut flora
antidiarrheal medications like loperamide only if needed
antibiotics only if bacterial infection confirmed
prevent spread to others through hygiene
seek medical care if symptoms persist beyond 48 hours

recommended_questions: How long have you noticed these symptoms?
Have you experienced unusual thirst or increased urination?
Have you noticed any unexplained weight changes?
Do you have family history of diabetes?
When was your last blood sugar check?
Have you experienced any vision changes?
Do you have any numbness or tingling in hands or feet?
Have you noticed slow healing of cuts or wounds?
What is your typical diet like?
How much physical activity do you get weekly?
Do you have high blood pressure?
What medications are you currently taking?
Have you been diagnosed with prediabetes previously?
Do you experience unusual fatigue?
treatments: dietary modifications reducing sugar and refined carbohydrates
regular physical activity at least 150 minutes per week
sulfonylureas or DPP-4 inhibitors if needed
annual eye screening for diabetic retinopathy
management of blood pressure and cholesterol

recommended_questions: How long have you been experiencing wheezing?
When do you experience shortness of breath? During activity or at rest?
Do you have a cough? Is it worse at night or during physical activity?
Do you experience chest tightness?
How often do you have asthma symptoms per week?
What triggers your symptoms?
Have you been diagnosed with asthma before?
Do family members have asthma or allergies?
Are you exposed to any allergens at home or work?
Are you a smoker or exposed to secondhand smoke?
Do you have any allergic rhinitis or eczema?
What medications have you tried?
Have you ever had a severe asthma attack?
Do you have a peak flow meter or spirometry results?
treatments: short-acting beta-2 agonists as rescue medication
regular monitoring with peak flow meter
asthma action plan with clear medication instructions

recommended_questions: How often do you experience heartburn?

Does lying down or bending over make it worse?

Do you have a chronic cough?

Do you smoke?
What is your typical meal size and timing?
Are you overweight?
Do you take any medications like NSAIDs or bisphosphonates?
Have you tried any treatments? What worked?
treatments: lifestyle modifications: smaller meals, elevation of head while sleeping
avoid trigger foods: spicy, fatty, acidic foods
surgery if refractory to medical management
